study_name: A Phase 2 double blind, randomized, placebo controlled study evaluating the effect of SAR443820 on serum neurofilament levels in participants with multiple sclerosis, followed by an open label long-term extension period
indication: Multiple Sclerosis (MS), including RRMS, SPMS, and PPMS
study_type: Phase 2, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, 2 parallel-arm study with open-label long-term extension (Part A: 48-week double-blind; Part B: open-label extension to Week 96)
protocol_version: Amended Clinical Trial Protocol 04, dated 13-December-2023; SAP Version 1, dated 12-Jul-2024 (VV-CLIN-0665575 1.0 / VV-CLIN-0702594 1.0)
visit_structure: Part A: Screening period up to 4 weeks, then double-blind treatment period 48 weeks with in-clinic visits at Day 1 (Baseline), Weeks 2, 6, 12, 24, 36, and 48. Part B: Open-label extension from Week 48 to Week 96 with in-clinic visits at Weeks 48, 52, 72, and 96; liver chemistry-only visits at Weeks 50, 54, 56, 58, 60, 62, 64, 66, 68, 70, 76, 80, 84, 88, and 92 (at local labs where available). Early discontinuation or safety follow-up visit up to 2 weeks after last dose of IMP for participants not entering Part B.

field_definitions.sNfL: Serum neurofilament light chain level; primary endpoint measured at baseline, Week 48 (Part A), and Week 96 (Part B)
field_definitions.EDSS_score: Expanded Disability Status Scale score; measure of disability progression
field_definitions.9HPT: 9-Hole Peg Test; quantitative test of upper extremity function (lower bound: 10 sec, upper bound: 300 sec); calculated as harmonic mean of dominant and non-dominant hand trials
field_definitions.T25FW: Timed 25-Foot Walk test; quantitative mobility and leg function test (lower bound: 2.2 sec, upper bound: 180 sec); average of 2 trials
field_definitions.CDP: Confirmed Disability Progression; EDSS increase of >=1.0 point from baseline if baseline <=5.5, or >=0.5 points if baseline >5.5, confirmed over 12 weeks (Part A) or 24 weeks (Part B)
field_definitions.CCDP: Composite Confirmed Disability Progression (EDSS-Plus); confirmed over 24 weeks in Part B
field_definitions.ARR: Annualized Relapse Rate in RMS population (relapsing SPMS and RRMS) up to Week 48
field_definitions.BVL: Brain Volume Loss; percent change from baseline as detected by brain MRI at 48 weeks
field_definitions.Gd_enhancing_T1_lesions: Cumulative number of new gadolinium-enhancing T1 hyperintense lesions as detected by MRI at Week 48
field_definitions.new_enlarging_T2_lesions: Cumulative number of new and/or enlarging T2 hyperintense lesions as detected by MRI at Week 48
field_definitions.slowly_expanding_lesions: Change from baseline in volume, number, and normalized intensity (T1) of slowly expanding lesions
field_definitions.phase_rim_lesions: Change from baseline in number of phase rim lesions (PRL) at Weeks 12, 24, 36, and 48 (3T capable sites only)
field_definitions.MSIS_29v2: Multiple Sclerosis Impact Scale version 2; physical impact score (items 1-20, transformed 0-100) and psychological impact score (items 21-29, transformed 0-100); higher scores indicate greater impact
field_definitions.MSWS_12: 12-Item MS Walking Scale; global score 0-100 (higher = better quality of life); sum of 12 items transformed to 0-100 scale
field_definitions.CSSRS: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale; baseline defined as worst assessment between past 6 months at screening and since last visit on Day 1
field_definitions.PK_plasma_concentration: Plasma concentration of SAR443820; summarized by arithmetic and geometric means per sampling time
field_definitions.ALT: Alanine aminotransferase; liver function test; AESI threshold >3x ULN
field_definitions.AST: Aspartate aminotransferase; liver function test; AESI threshold >3x ULN
field_definitions.total_bilirubin: Total bilirubin; liver function test; AESI threshold >2x ULN
field_definitions.rbc: Red blood cell count; hematology parameter
field_definitions.wbc: White blood cell count; hematology parameter
field_definitions.neutrophils: White blood cell differential; hematology parameter
field_definitions.lymphocytes: White blood cell differential; hematology parameter
field_definitions.monocytes: White blood cell differential; hematology parameter
field_definitions.basophils: White blood cell differential; hematology parameter
field_definitions.eosinophils: White blood cell differential; hematology parameter
field_definitions.bun: Blood urea nitrogen; clinical chemistry; renal function
field_definitions.alp: Alkaline phosphatase; clinical chemistry; liver function
field_definitions.direct_bilirubin: Direct bilirubin; clinical chemistry; liver function
field_definitions.hbsag: Hepatitis B surface antigen; hepatitis screen
field_definitions.hcv_antibody: Hepatitis C virus antibody; hepatitis screen
field_definitions.urinalysis: Quantitative urinalysis: pH, specific gravity, proteins, glucose, blood, ketones, bilirubin, urobilinogen, nitrite, leukocyte esterase (by dipstick)
field_definitions.ecg_qtc_bazett: Corrected QTc interval per Bazett formula
field_definitions.ecg_qtc_fridericia: Corrected QTc interval per Fridericia formula
field_definitions.ms_clinical_subtype: MS clinical subtype: RRMS, SPMS, or PPMS; randomization stratification factor
field_definitions.background_dmt: Background disease-modifying therapy; concomitant or absent
field_definitions.bmi: Body mass index; used as subgroup factor (baseline BMI <median vs >=median)

validation_rules.sNfL_baseline: Baseline NfL defined as lower of screening and Day 1 values if Gd-enhancing T1 lesion count at Day 1 > 0; otherwise average of screening and Day 1 values prior to first dose
validation_rules.EDSS_baseline: Baseline EDSS defined as average of screening and Day 1 visit values prior to first dose; if one value missing, non-missing value used
validation_rules.primary_endpoint_evaluable: Participant must have a baseline and at least 1 post-baseline sNfL assessment before IMP discontinuation and before permanent modification of background DMT
validation_rules.9HPT_bounds: Values below lower bound (10 sec) set to 10 sec; values above upper bound (300 sec) set to 300 sec; if participant cannot complete due to physical limitation, maximum time (300 sec) used; if only 1 trial done for a hand, average replaced by available trial; if only one hand assessed, overall 9-HPT considered missing
validation_rules.T25FW_bounds: Values below lower bound (2.2 sec) set to 2.2 sec; values above upper bound (180 sec) set to 180 sec; if participant cannot complete due to physical limitation, maximum time (180 sec) used; missing values for other reasons not imputed
validation_rules.LLOQ_handling: Data below LLOQ replaced by half of LLOQ; data above ULOQ replaced by ULOQ value for quantitative analysis
validation_rules.PK_LLOQ: For drug-treated participants, concentration values below LLOQ replaced by one-half of LLOQ
validation_rules.CDP_confirmation_partA: Confirmed disability progression on EDSS confirmed over at least 12 weeks in Part A
validation_rules.CDP_confirmation_partB: Confirmed disability progression on EDSS, 9-HPT (>=20% increase), T25-FW (>=20% increase), and CCDP confirmed over at least 24 weeks in Part B
validation_rules.EDSS_CDP_threshold: Increase of >=1.0 point from baseline EDSS if baseline <=5.5; increase of >=0.5 points if baseline >5.5
validation_rules.death_MS: Death due to MS considered confirmed disability progression regardless of baseline EDSS or change in EDSS
validation_rules.death_other: Death for reasons other than MS: participant event time censored at date of last EDSS assessment
validation_rules.MSIS29v2_imputation: Missing items imputed by domain average if at least 50% of items completed (>=10 physical, >=5 psychological)
validation_rules.MSWS12_imputation: Missing items imputed by average of available items if at least 50% completed (>=6 items)
validation_rules.analysis_window_tie: If multiple assessments equidistant from target study day, value after targeted study day used; if multiple valid values same day, first value of day selected
validation_rules.ALT_AESI_threshold: ALT increase >3x ULN triggers AESI reporting via dedicated CRF page
validation_rules.DILI_threshold: ALT or AST >3x ULN with total bilirubin >2x ULN triggers drug-induced liver injury analysis
validation_rules.subgroup_combination: Subgroup categories with fewer than 10% of participants combined with other categories; for factors with only two categories, no subgroup analysis if fewer than 10% in one category
validation_rules.randomization_multiple: For participants randomized more than once, only data from first randomization used in any analysis population
validation_rules.CSSRS_baseline: Baseline C-SSRS defined as worst assessment between past 6 months evaluation at screening and since last visit evaluation on Day 1

categorical: CSSRS categories (no suicidal ideation/behavior, suicidal ideation, suicidal behavior)
time_to_event: time to CDP (12-week confirmed, Part A)
time to CDP (24-week confirmed, Part B)
time to 20% increase in T25-FW (Part B)
time to CCDP (24-week confirmed, Part B)

inclusion: MS clinical subtype of RRMS, SPMS, or PPMS
Clinical status evaluated based on EDSS during screening
Suitable for participation based on safety screening evaluations during screening period
exclusion: Screen failure: consented but not subsequently randomized based on eligibility criteria
Participants exposed to study intervention before or without being randomized are excluded from all analysis populations

medication_guidelines.study_medication: SAR443820 20 mg once daily (QD) orally. Part A: randomized 1:1 to SAR443820 20 mg QD (n=84) or matching placebo QD (n=84). Part B: all participants receive SAR443820 20 mg QD open-label.
prohibited: Permanent modification of background disease-modifying therapy (DMT) during the study period (triggers intercurrent event handling under hypothetical strategy for primary endpoint)
IMP discontinuation (triggers intercurrent event handling under hypothetical strategy for primary endpoint)
allowed: Background disease-modifying therapy (DMT) on top of study intervention where applicable
Rescue therapy intermittently limited to 2 to 5 days, after which original background DMT is restored at pre-rescue dose (not considered permanent modification of background DMT)
Concomitant medications as recorded in prior or concomitant medications CRF pages
